1: Which teacheth my hands to war, and and my fingers to fight:
2: Who subdueth my people under me.
3: Or Or the son of man, that thou makest account of him!
4: His days are are as a shadow that passeth away.
5: Touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.
6: Shoot out thine arrows, and destroy them.
7: Rid me, and deliver me out of great waters, from the hand of strange children;
8: And their right hand is is a right hand of falsehood.
9: Upon a psaltery and and an instrument of ten strings will I sing praises unto thee.
10: Who delivereth David his servant from the hurtful sword.
11: Whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is is a right hand of falsehood:
12: That That our daughters may be may be as corner stones, polished after after the similitude of a palace:
13: That That our sheep may bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our streets:
14: That there be That there be no breaking in, nor going out; That there be there be no complaining in our streets.
15: Yea Yea , happy is that is that people, whose God is is the LORD LORD .
